name: Oatmeal Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup egg equivalent (commercial replacer, two eggs or 1/4 cup "egg beaters")
1 cup cake flour (all-purpose is okay but not as tender)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Line two baking sheets with parchment paper or coat with non-stick spray.
3. Toasting oats: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and add half the oats. Toast, stirring frequently until the oats begin to color and become fragrant (about 15 minutes). Repeat with the remaining oats.
4. In a large bowl, whisk together brown sugar, applesauce, egg equivalent, and vanilla extract. Stir in raisins.
5. In a medium bowl, stir together cake flour, whole wheat flour, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt.
6. Add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture and mix just until blended.
7. Drop tablespoonfuls of dough onto prepared baking sheets, spacing cookies about 1.5 inches apart.
8. Bake for 15 minutes, or until lightly browned. Transfer cookies to a wire rack to cool.
9. Makes 3 dozen cookies.
Storage: No-fat-added cookies do not store well. They become stale quickly and stick together if stacked. As soon as they are cool, wrap the cookies, layers separated by waxed paper, and freeze. To serve, simply let them thaw to room temperature.
